originalBehavior: The published Musicmap page boots a large SVG graph with zoom controls, search, genre navigation, and a right-side genre pane with descriptive copy and playlist context.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published graph shell, local search payload, zoom-to-genre behavior, and right-side genre pane while moving provenance into the shared footer.
notes: The route stays on the vendored published bundle instead of reauthoring the map.
Search and genre hydration rely on the vendored JSON payload inside the local route tree.
evidence: Music smoke coverage opens search results, loads a genre pane, and verifies the local song list and playlist links.
The local graph shell exposes the same zoom buttons, search controls, and genre side pane structure as the published page.

originalBehavior: The published route includes shell-level banners, privacy and about panes, social buttons, analytics hooks, remote fonts, and other non-core page chrome around the map.
localStatus: The local route archives the first-party static shell assets locally, strips the non-essential promo and tracking surfaces from the public body, and rewrites the shell to local metadata and footer-only provenance.
notes: The vendored static inventory includes 5 files under musicmap/assets, plus main.css, main.bundle.js, master-genrelist.json, favicon.ico, and apple-touch-icon.png.
Remote font imports were replaced with local font-family fallbacks in the vendored CSS.
evidence: The public route no longer ships the upstream store banner, privacy banner, disclaimer panes, social buttons, Google Analytics, or Facebook SDK in the body shell.
The public-surface audit enforces footer-only provenance and Musicmap-specific shell cleanup on the route HTML.

originalBehavior: The published Musicmap runtime cues remote playlists and exposes direct YouTube and Spotify playlist surfaces inside the genre pane.
localStatus: The local route keeps playlist context but gates playback behind explicit user clicks that create only YouTube or Spotify embeds, with no eager remote media shell before interaction.
notes: This exception is scoped to Musicmap only; the rest of the music family still requires fully local media archives.
Allowed remote playback providers are YouTube and Spotify only, and only after user action.
evidence: Music smoke coverage verifies the route stays remote-clean before interaction, then creates an allowed deferred embed after a user click.
The route converts playlist actions into iframe embeds instead of preserving the upstream eager iframe API flow.
